context.description: A philosophical discussion asks whether a permanent lucid waking state would overwhelm the brain, and focuses on forgetting as the main barrier to inquiry. It also examines how language limits shared understanding when concepts, labels, or words are missing.
normalization_notes: Normalized raw category and subcategory to the closest allowed book body content theoretical discussion.

rows.paragraph_text: A philosophical passage questions the mental strain of permanent lucid waking and whether it could lead to madness. It argues that forgetting is the greatest barrier to investigating such experiences, and that communication breaks down when people lack shared terms or categories.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion book page on consciousness, forgetting, and language barriers in philosophical reflection
